summary: Shipped the three-person module Hello World walkthrough (docs/tutorials/module-team-hello-world.md) and the reusable module-workflow review prompt (docs/tutorials/module-team-hello-world-review-prompt.md); linked both from every onboarding surface (root README, quick-start, extension README); landed the 094-deferred getting-started.md.template re-cut plus a monorepo-ci.yml.template permissions fix (goldens + bundled dist regenerated); updated the 0.42.0 CHANGELOG pre-publish note (only the getting-started.png screenshot retake remains, operator-gated). The review prompt was dogfooded eight times against a planted-violation scratch repo via its own routed recipe - the planted violation FAILed with exact citations in every run, and the honest-ADVISORY caps behaved as designed. Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-6, anthropic excluded) ran 18 rounds (1 void by empty evidence - orchestrator process error, --diff-base repointed thereafter): 39 blocking findings, ALL accepted and fixed in flight, zero disputed; the loop terminated VERIFIED zero-findings at R18, the first round graded under the operator's consequence-based severity rubric (set 2026-07-12 with a 21-round hard cap). Verification spend $4.88; session routed total $5.25. Suite green at close: pytest 2922/6 skipped, extension unit 1487, Playwright 26, tsc clean. UAT: requiresUAT suggested was NOT armed (autonomous session; no operator opt-in); the walkthrough is authored to the UAT instruction bar and doubles as the operator's optional hands-on walk. Follow-ons recorded: the phased-verification framework set (discovery fan-out + scoped remediation review + consequence-graded severity in the shipped template - primary evidence is this session's loop), the routed next-set ranking in s1-next-set-analysis.json (077 UAT-redo first), and the screenshot retake.
